liveEvidenceEndpoints.name: failed-event-summary
liveEvidenceEndpoints.purpose: Aggregate replay backlog and approval status signals.

liveEvidenceEndpoints.name: replay-readiness
liveEvidenceEndpoints.purpose: Explain whether one failed event is eligible for replay.

liveEvidenceEndpoints.name: approval-status
liveEvidenceEndpoints.purpose: Expose stored approval status and approval/replay eligibility digests.

liveEvidenceEndpoints.name: replay-execution-contract
liveEvidenceEndpoints.purpose: Describe the preconditions and expected side effects of real replay.

liveEvidenceEndpoints.name: replay-attempts
liveEvidenceEndpoints.purpose: Read replay attempt audit history for one failed event.

liveEvidenceEndpoints.name: replay-approval-history
liveEvidenceEndpoints.purpose: Read replay approval request/review audit history for one failed event.

normalizationRules: operator id must be present, stripped, and truncated to 80 chars
operator role must be present, stripped, upper-cased, allow-listed, and truncated to 80 chars
action-specific role checks run after the global allow-list
operator-context endpoint exposes allowed and denied actions
productionAuthGaps: Java does not validate JWT/session cookies/external IdP signatures yet.
Upstream gateway/control plane must prevent client-side spoofing of X-Operator-* headers.
Header-derived identity is rehearsal/audit evidence, not final production auth.
Real replay still requires approval status, action role, non-blank reason, RabbitMQ outbox readiness.

productionReadinessNotes: This index is read-only and does not execute replay.
Static samples are fixtures for smoke, diagnostics, and control-plane contract alignment.
Live endpoints should be queried before POST /api/v1/failed-events/{id}/replay.
The real replay POST still owns the final approval, role, request, and RabbitMQ checks.
Operator headers are precheck context, not production authentication.
